pr.title: Match itzgshell, itlugia, ithouou, ftKirby, ftPurin, grpushon functions
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Matched a batch of previously stubbed item, fighter, and stage functions across Green Shell/Zelda Shell item logic, Lugia/Ho-Oh item animation callbacks, Kirby copied-special collision transitions, Jigglypuff/Purin Rollout startup animation, and a PushOn ground collision callback. The PR also refined item variable layout in itCommonItems.h and updated the grpushon callback prototype with concrete Ground, CollData, and mpLib_GroundEnum types.
what_changed: Implemented itZrshell_UnkMotion1_Anim, itZrshell_UnkMotion3_Anim, and itZrshell_UnkMotion8_Anim in itzgshell.c, replacing placeholder comments with timer/flag-driven item animation logic.
Added a local itGShell_Attrs struct in itzgshell.c for shell special attributes used by matched animation logic.

Expanded itGShell_ItemVars in itCommonItems.h by replacing broad padding with concrete float fields at xDF4, xDFC, xE04 and bitfields at xE08 and xE1C while preserving the asserted struct size of 88 bytes.
Implemented itLugia_UnkMotion4_Anim and itHouou_UnkMotion3_Anim using similar JObj child lookup, it_8027A160 update, animation-state change via Item_80268E5C, and hitlag callback reset patterns.

Implemented several Kirby copied-special collision functions in ftKb_Init.c: ftKb_NsSpecialNEnd_Coll, ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialAirNCancel_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialAirN_Coll, and ftKb_DkSpecialAirNFull_Coll.
Implemented ftPr_SpecialNStart_Anim in ftPr_SpecialN.c, moving setupPurinCallbacks above it so startup can transition into ftPr_MS_SpecialNLoop and install callbacks when animation frames end.
Implemented fn_802190A0 in grpushon.c as a PushOn ground collision callback that sets gp->u.map.xC4_b0 when selected collision flag bits and ground enum constraints match.
Updated grpushon.h to include mp/forward.h and replace fn_802190A0's placeholder UNK signature with void fn_802190A0(Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum).
smart_moves: Used existing character behavior as templates for Kirby copied specials, which is especially effective because Kirby's copy ability code often mirrors the source character's already-decompiled special move logic.

Kept matched Kirby collision transitions compact and pattern-based: test ground/air state, call the corresponding common ground/air transition helper, preserve cur_anim_frame, and call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with precise motion IDs and flags.

For Lugia and Ho-Oh item animation callbacks, reused the same structural pattern: tolerate a NULL JObj, operate on the child JObj when present, call it_8027A160, and restore hitlag pause/resume callbacks after the item animation update.
Introduced only the needed itGShell_ItemVars fields and bitfields instead of fully naming or over-specifying the whole item variable block, while maintaining the STATIC_ASSERT on size.
Converted a stage callback from UNK_PARAMS to a concrete typed prototype, improving future callsite readability and reducing placeholder dependency.
decomp_lessons: Kirby copy ability files can be high-yield targets for AI-assisted or template-based matching because many functions are copy-pasted or lightly adapted from the original fighter's matched implementation.
When matching item callbacks, exposing exact item variable offsets as fields and bitfields can unblock clean C expressions while still preserving layout with padding and STATIC_ASSERTs.

Animation callback matches often require seemingly minor ordering details such as inline helper placement, callback assignment timing, cur_anim_frame reset, animation-rate change, and velocity zeroing.
For item JObj child handling, explicitly checking for NULL before accessing jobj->child can be necessary for matching and documents the original control flow.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete function signatures is useful when the implementation reveals parameter roles, especially for callbacks such as ground collision handlers.
Bitfield access in decomp may need to mirror packed flag extraction closely; fn_802190A0 uses a byte cast and shift/mask expression on coll->x34_flags to match the original.
naming_conventions: Kept existing symbol prefixes by subsystem: ftKb_ for Kirby, ftPr_ for Purin/Jigglypuff, itZrshell_ for Zelda/green shell item motion callbacks, itLugia_ and itHouou_ for Pokémon item callbacks, and grPushOn_ for PushOn stage code.
Preserved address-style unnamed function naming for fn_802190A0 rather than inventing a semantic public name, while adding a descriptive comment for its callback purpose.
Used offset-based temporary struct field names such as xDF4, xDFC, xE04, xE08_b0, and xE1C_b0 where semantic names were not yet established.
The PR retained a TODO for unnamed motion-state flags in ftPr_SpecialNStart_Anim rather than prematurely naming the bitmask.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Template matching from already-decompiled source-character specials was explicitly called out in review as a productive strategy for Kirby copied specials.

Verified additional copy-ability functions with the project verification tooling; the review excerpt shows `tools.py verify ftKb_LgSpecialN_Coll` reporting 100% matched, though those exact functions are not shown in the supplied diff excerpt.
Used exact motion-state constants and flags in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including values such as 0x1BA, 0x1B6, 0x1C9, 0x1CA, 0x1CB, 0x0C4C5080U, and 0x0C4D508EU.
Preserved original float behaviors with explicit values such as 1.0f, 0.0f, 0.0001f, and decrement-by-1.0f timer updates.
For grpushon, matched low-level flag extraction by casting the CollData flag field to u8 pointer, shifting by 3, masking with 0xF, and comparing to 1.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that this Kirby file is a strong candidate for AI assistance because much of it is copy-pasted from other already-decompiled characters, and asked the author to investigate further.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that the pattern appeared valid and planned to leave the agent running; shared verification output showing build success and 100% matches for additional Kirby copy-ability functions.

follow_up_queries: Find additional ftKb_Init.c Kirby copied-special stubs whose source-character equivalents are already matched.
Search for other item variable structs with broad padding near known accessed offsets that can be safely split into concrete fields and bitfields.
Look for Lugia and Ho-Oh callback pairs that share the same animation/update/hitlag pattern.
Audit grpushon callbacks for more UNK_PARAMS signatures that can be typed using Ground, CollData, or mpLib_GroundEnum.
Identify remaining motion-state flag TODOs in Purin/Jigglypuff special move code that could be given named constants.
